mechanism_narrative: An observable downward trend in the federal funds rate over the prior 3 months reduces discount rates on future cash flows and signals higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window where the 3-month FEDFUNDS trend is downward, walk-forward Rank IC for the signal is < 0.03 or average 20-day forward returns in triggered instances are not significantly > 0 (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) increases near-term corporate revenue growth expectations and liquidity for equities, producing positive S&P500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In a backtest, instances with positive month-over-month RSAFS growth do not produce average positive 5-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE drops > 0.2 percentage points) tightens labor markets, signaling stronger aggregate demand and higher short-term equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: When the trigger occurs, 20-day forward returns are not reliably positive: average return ≤ 0 or Rank IC < 0.02 across triggered instances within any 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO decline > 1%) signals weakening supply-side activity and falling corporate output, producing negative S&P500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not show negative 5-day forward returns on average (mean ≥ 0) or Rank IC ≥ 0.02 is not maintained over any 12-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI (CPIAUCSL YoY) is elevated (> 2.5%) and the Federal Funds rate has risen over the prior 3 months (> 0.25pp), the joint signal indicates active monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in prior 3 months
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions hold, average 60-day forward returns are non-negative or Rank IC < 0.03 across triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent noticeable declines in payrolls (PAYEMS drop > 50k in 1 month) and retail sales (RSAFS falls > 1% month-over-month) indicate demand collapse and reduced earnings, producing negative S&P500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls > 50 thousand in 1 month AND RSAFS falls > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce non-negative average 20-day forward returns or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 2-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising single-family housing starts (HOUST YoY growth > 5%) increase construction-related earnings and consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when short-term rates are very low (FEDFUNDS < 1%).
failure_condition: When the joint trigger occurs, average 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 across triggered instances in any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-month positive trend in industrial production (INDPRO increases month-over-month for 2 months) reflects improving supply and demand congruence and raises short-term earnings prospects, producing positive S&P500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances with INDPRO up 2 months do not yield positive average 20-day returns (mean ≤ 0) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
